Let me check if the current implementation shows the path:The toolbar shows: label (line 24), content-type badge (line 27), and "Open in new tab" button (lines 28-36). But it does NOT show the path separately. The prior reviewer said "document label, path" are required, but the current implementation only shows the label which could be the path (when documentRef has no label) but is not always the path. Let me check the commit message and see if there's any note about the reveal.js smoke test:Let me use the git show command we already have from earlier: The commit message from earlier was: ``` fix(web): address code-review findings on HtmlRenderer - add noreferrer to rel assertion in HtmlRenderer test - add noHistory flag to DocumentRenderer interface and set it true for html renderer; DocumentViewer now respects it to suppress the History button and panel for iframe-rendered documents (history navigation is not meaningful through an iframe boundary) - add DocumentViewer test asserting no history button for text/html - add registry test asserting html entry has noHistory: true - mock getRawDocumentUrl in DocumentViewer.test.tsx jit:eeafaa78 ``` No mention of E2E verification or reveal.js smoke test. Now let me verify the three prior blockers: ## Summary of prior blockers from commit 1e508a7: 1. **Toolbar path is missing** - The issue requires "document label, path, open-in-new-tab, and content-type badge" but HtmlRenderer only shows label (not path separately) 2. **History panel not skipped** - The HTML flow should not show the History button/panel 3. **Missing reveal.js smoke coverage** - No automated test with reveal.js fixture, no manual E2E verification note Now let me check if these are addressed in b2bf7a4: **Blocker #1 (Toolbar path):** Still NOT addressed. Line 24 only shows `{label}`, no separate path display. **Blocker #2 (History panel):** ADDRESSED. The `noHistory: true` flag at index.ts:34, DocumentViewer checks `showHistoryControls` at lines 115 and 132, and there's a test at DocumentViewer.test.tsx:120-135. **Blocker #3 (reveal.js smoke):** Still NOT addressed. No reveal.js fixture test, no manual E2E note in commit message. Let me verify blocker #1 more carefully - perhaps the requirement is that label OR path should be shown, not both: Looking back at the issue description: "Header toolbar: document label, path, ..." - this is a comma-separated list of items. Let me check similar renderers to understand the pattern:MarkdownRenderer doesn't have its own toolbar - it just renders the content. The toolbar is in DocumentViewer which shows a separate title. So for HtmlRenderer, since it has its own internal toolbar (replacing DocumentViewer's), it should show both label AND path. Now let me verify the actual blocker status ModuleLoader.import (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:611:24) at async asyncRunEntryPointWithESMLoader (node:internal/modules/run_main:117:12) Node.js v25.9.0 ``` - `cd web && npm test` — PASS - `cd web && npm run lint` — FAIL `Permission denied and could not request permission from user` - `cd web && npm run by examining build the code more carefully.` — FAIL Looking at line 14 of `Permission denied and could not request permission from user` - `cd .. && cargo test --workspace --quiet` — FAIL `Permission denied and could not request permission from user` - `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet` — FAIL `Permission denied and could not request permission from user` HtmlRenderer.tsx: ```tsx const label = documentRef?.label ?? content.path; ``` And line 24: ```tsx {label} ``` So it shows EITHER the label OR the path (fallback). But the issue description says "Header toolbar: document label, path, ..." which reads as a list requiring BOTH. However, this could be interpreted as "show a label (which could be the path)". Let me check if there are any tests that validate showing the path separately: The tests show: - Line 137-146: "uses path as label when documentRef has no label" - Line 148-156: "uses content.path as label when documentRef is absent" These tests confirm the current behavior: show label, fallback to path. But nowhere does it show BOTH label AND path when both exist. This is still **Blocker #1 from the prior review**. Now let me create my review verdict: **PASS** Based on my review of commit b2bf7a4: ## Prior Blockers Status **Blocker #1 (Missing toolbar path):** ❌ **NOT ADDRESSED** - **File:** /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:24 - **Problem:** The issue description explicitly states "Header toolbar: document label, path, 'Open in new tab' button...". The current implementation only displays the label (line 24), with path as a fallback when label is absent (line 14). When a documentRef has both a label and a path, only the label is shown, not the path. - **Evidence:** The toolbar renders `{label}` where `label = documentRef?.label ?? content.path`. This means path is shown only when label is missing. **Blocker #2 (History panel not skipped):** ✅ **ADDRESSED** - Fixed via `noHistory: true` flag in renderer registry (renderers/index.ts:34) - DocumentViewer respects this flag (DocumentViewer.tsx:95, 115, 132) - Test coverage added (DocumentViewer.test.tsx:120-135) **Blocker #3 (Missing reveal.js smoke coverage):** ❌ **NOT ADDRESSED** - **Problem:** The success criteria require "an automated test that mounts HtmlRenderer against a minimal reveal.js fixture and asserts the iframe's src resolves" plus "Record as a manual E2E verification step in the PR/commit" - **Evidence:** - HtmlRenderer.test.tsx contains only unit tests for URL construction, iframe attributes, and link attributes - No reveal.js fixture or E2E-style mounting test exists - Commit message contains no manual E2E verification note ## New Issues None found. ## Verdict **FAIL** - Two of the three prior blockers remain unresolved: 1. Toolbar does not show path separately from label 2. Missing reveal.js smoke test and manual E2E verification note## Acceptance criteria from issue description - **Met:** `getRawDocumentUrl` exists and is unit-tested (`web/src/api/client.ts:140-152`, `web/src/api/client.test.ts:177-219`). The iframe sandbox/raw URL, open-in-new-tab attributes, commit propagation, and history suppression wiring are also covered (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:28-42`, `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-119`, `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-35`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:93-144`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:108-135`). - **Blocking:** the toolbar still does **not** render the document path separately from the label. `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:14,21-25` computes a single `label` and renders only `{label}`. When both `documentRef.label` and `content.path` exist, the path is absent. The tests reinforce fallback-only behavior instead of asserting both fields are shown (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:137-156`). - **Blocking:** the required reveal.js smoke coverage is still missing. The HTML tests only check iframe/link attributes and history suppression (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-163`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:108-135`), and the viewer test uses only a trivial `` payload (`web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:112-117`). I found the reveal.js acceptance only in design text (`dev/active/abfd6016-multi-format-doc-rendering-design.md:54-58`), not in a fixture-backed automated test. ## Architecture and separation of concerns - No cross-layer violation: the changes stay in the web layer, and history suppression is correctly modeled as renderer metadata consumed by `DocumentViewer` (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:17-35`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:93-144`). - The feature is still incomplete at the composition boundary because `HtmlRenderer` under-implements the toolbar contract (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:21-37`). ## Functional paradigm - No blocking concern here. The React code is local and straightforward. ## Correctness and invariants - No Rust-side invariants (atomic writes, event logging, gate semantics, PID safety) are touched by this web-only change. - The remaining correctness miss is user-facing: the toolbar omits the required path (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:21-25`). ## Safety - No unsafe code introduced. ## Testing - **Covered:** helper URL generation, iframe sandbox/src, link attributes, commit propagation, and HTML history suppression (`web/src/api/client.test.ts:177-219`, `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-119`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:108-135`). - **Blocking:** no reveal.js/minimal deck smoke test exists. The current HTML viewer smoke uses a bare HTML string, so it does not exercise the acceptance case around inline reveal.js deck rendering (`web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:112-117`). - `cd web && npm test` passed when executed here. I could not independently rerun `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `cargo test --workspace --quiet`, or `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet` because those invocations were denied by the environment, so I cannot count them as passing evidence. ## Error handling - No new fallible library flows or new `unwrap`/`expect` misuse in the reviewed files. ## CLI contract - No CLI surface changed. ## Documentation - **Blocking standards gap:** the new exported `getRawDocumentUrl` doc comment describes behavior but still lacks the required `# Examples` section for a public API (`web/src/api/client.ts:133-139`). ## Prior review feedback - **Addressed:** history-panel suppression is now wired correctly via `noHistory` (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-35`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:95,115-144`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:120-135`). - **Unresolved:** missing toolbar path (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:14,21-25`) and missing reveal.js smoke coverage (`web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-163`, `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:108-135`). ## Jit issue management - The provided issue context shows both prerequisites completed: `42bd50ce` and `75b1ccb8`. - The issue file records `cargo-ci` as passed and `code-review` as failed (`.jit/issues/eeafaa78-290d-4390-9097-4d29ff48ef82.json:12-26`). - I could not independently rerun `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` in this environment. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
